caption: Aggregated Finding
description: An Aggregated Finding consolidates other finding references into an aggregate finding, via a list of finding_info objects. The class implements the same structure as Incident Finding but without incident semantics. Apply the Incident profile to add the incident attributes when the aggregated finding is declared or promoted as an incident.
name: aggregated_finding

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the finding activity. Use 1 (Create) when a finding is first generated, 2 (Update) when an existing finding is modified (e.g., severity change, new evidence), and 3 (Close) when a finding is resolved or dismissed.

2.caption: Update
2.description: An aggregated finding was updated, including adding, updating, or closing any of the aggregates.

3.caption: Close
3.description: An aggregated finding was closed, along with all of the aggregates.

activity_name.description: The finding activity name, as defined by the activity_id. When activity_id is 99 (Other), this must contain the source-specific activity label.

attacks.description: An array describing the tactics, techniques & sub-techniques of the aggregated findings; a union of those associated across all aggregated findings.

desc.description: The short description of the aggregated finding.

end_time.description: The time of the most recent event or finding that contributed to this aggregated finding.

finding_info_list.description: A list of finding_info objects that represent the union of aggregated findings. Each element of the list summarizes a finding and the uid attribute references the full finding.

notes.caption: Notes
notes.description: Additional notes related to the finding. Each note in the array can include a comment, the user who made the comment, the time when the note was created, and the time when the note was last modified (typically by the same user). Notes can be used to provide additional context, explanations, or observations by analysts related to the incident over time.

start_time.description: The time of the earliest event or finding that contributed to this aggregated finding.

status.description: The aggregated finding lifecycle status label, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. When status_id is 99 (Other), this must contain the source-specific status label.

status_id.description: The normalized aggregated finding lifecycle status identifier. This tracks the management workflow from initial triage through resolution. Producers should set this to reflect the current state in their management system.

1.caption: New
1.description: The management system has created, or received the aggregated finding but has not assigned it to an agent.

2.caption: In Progress
2.description: The aggregated finding has been assigned to an agent but has not been resolved. The agent is actively working with the user to diagnose and triage the underlying findings.

3.caption: On Hold
3.description: Some or all of the aggregated findings require more information or response from the user or from a third party.

4.caption: Resolved
4.description: The aggregated findings have been resolved.

5.caption: Closed
5.description: The aggregated findings have been resolved and no further action is necessary.

6.caption: Promoted
6.description: Some or all of the aggregated findings have been promoted to an incident. Apply the Incident profile or create an Incident Finding.

time.description: The aggregated finding creation time — when the finding was first created, not when the underlying finding or finding activity occurred. For the time range of contributing findings, use start_time and end_time.
